name: Thai Seafood Casserole with Basil
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Large shrimp, shelled and deveined
1/2 lb Large cleaned squid, cut crosswise into 1-inch rings
1 lb Fresh fish fillets, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 tbsp Golden Mountain Sauce (made with Thai-style soy sauce)
1 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
Golden Mountain Sauce is prepared with Thai-style soy sauce, which may be found in Southeast Asian markets.
1. In a bowl, cover the mung bean noodles with warm water and soak until soft and pliable, about 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
2. Clean and prepare the live crab according to the following instructions (if using cooked crab, follow after steaming step).
- Shell and devein the shrimp; pat dry.
- Clean squid: Cut the body crosswise into 1-inch rings; set aside the rings and cleaned tentacles.
3. In a mortar or spice mill, pound or grind the peppercorns, coriander root, and garlic into a paste.
4. In a bowl, mix together the sugar, Golden Mountain Sauce, soy sauces, oyster sauce, rice wine, and sesame oil.
5. Add oil to the clay pot or Dutch oven. Set over medium heat.
6. Add the reserved coriander-garlic-pepper paste to the oil; sauté lightly.
7. Increase to high heat.
8. Add ginger, chiles, and crab pieces (add cooked crab later); toss and brown for a minute.
9. Add shrimp and green onions to the crab mixture; stir-fry for 30 seconds.
10. Pour in the soy sauce mixture and mung bean noodles; toss and mix together.
11. Stir in water, cover the pot, reduce heat to medium, and simmer for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally. If noodles seem dry, add more water.
12. Increase to medium-high heat, add fish, squid, and cooked crab, mixing with the noodles. Top with basil leaves. Cover and cook for 3 to 5 minutes longer.
13. Check for doneness. Bring the clay pot to the table and serve hot with rice and vegetables.
To clean and prepare the crab:
- Scrub the underside and in between claws and legs to loosen sand and grit. Rinse well with cold water.
- Plunge into boiling water for a minute to kill it immediately. Remove and cool.
- Disjoint and crack the crab by holding the shell portion of the crab in one hand, gathering the legs and claws on one side, and gently pulling and tugging until the body and legs loosen as a whole section from the shell.
- On both sides of the body are featherly gills. Pull off and discard these gills.
- The tail piece is located on the underside of the body. Lift up the flap with the tip of a knife, grasp it, twist off, and discard.
- Scrub the exposed area clean. Remove legs and claws by gently pulling and bending them backward against their natural bend; twist to free them.
- Chops the chest-body section into two equal pieces; chop each piece crosswise into three equal pieces.
- Crack the mid-section and joint of each leg using a light hammer or metal nut cracker; set aside the pieces.
To clean and prepare squid:
- Grasp the body with one hand, and pull the head and tentacles away until separated from the body.
- Remove quill, transparent cartilage, and discard them.
- Peel off the dotted skin from the body and discard.
- Rinse the body with cold water.
- Cut between the eye and tentacles; discard the head.
- Squeeze the thick center part of the tentacles to dislodge the hard beak; rinse with cold water. Set aside.
